reason: Applying a GRUB2 boot loader password modifies the bootloader configuration. If the password is set incorrectly or forgotten, it can prevent the system from booting, resulting in a complete lockout. This aligns with the policy that bootloader changes are considered too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; Potential loss of boot access if password is incorrect or forgotten; Requires physical console or rescue media to recover; Bootloader configuration changes can impact system startup
